title: Photovoltaics Report
why_relevant: Industry-canonical PV market context: cumulative installed capacity, c-Si market share, learning curve, cost structure. Cited by every PV review; provides baseline to motivate Cu replacing Ag.

title: International Technology Roadmap for Photovoltaic (ITRPV) 15th Edition - Results 2023
why_relevant: Authoritative industry roadmap: per-cell Ag laydown projections (HJT 2023 ~17 mg/cell heading to ~5 mg/cell by 2034), Cu plating adoption forecast for SHJ, n-type/TOPCon/HJT market share. Direct primary source for $/cell economic justification of the hypothesis.

title: Technological learning for resource efficient terawatt scale photovoltaics
why_relevant: Canonical TW-PV resource-bottleneck framing paper (Energy & Environmental Science, OA). Quantifies that without learning curve continuation, Ag/In/Bi reserves limit deployment well below 70 TW. Core motivation for the entire hypothesis.

title: Future challenges for photovoltaic manufacturing at the terawatt level
why_relevant: Verlinden 2020 J. Renewable Sustainable Energy. Establishes the silver-supply ceiling argument for c-Si PV at TW scale and explicitly motivates plated Cu metallization as the resolution. High-impact, frequently cited.

title: Aging tests of mini-modules with copper-plated heterojunction solar cells and pattern-transfer-printing of copper paste
authors: et al. (CSEM, CEA INES, AMAT, DR Utilight)
why_relevant: Most directly comparable result: SHJ Cu-plated mini-modules tested at 0/1000/2000 h damp-heat. Reports ~5% degradation at 2700 h (glass-glass, no edge sealing) and explicit comparison to Ag baseline. Critical for assessing whether the 1000 h Δη ≤ 0.5 %abs threshold is achievable.

title: TaiyangNews Heterojunction Solar Technology 2023 Report
why_relevant: Industry survey listing every SHJ Cu-plating commercializer and their efficiency claims (Maxwell, SunDrive at 26.41%, Tongwei, Huasun, GS-Solar, Hanergy, Risen). Maps the competitive landscape directly relevant to novelty QC.

title: Stability of Mini-Modules with Copper-Plated Heterojunction Solar Cells (MI Workshop slides)
why_relevant: Companion presentation to Lachowicz EPJ-PV 2024 with extended damp-heat curves and SEM/EDX of Cu finger morphology. Useful for failure-mode entities (Ni-barrier-pinholes, Cu-oxidation-during-damp-heat).

title: Achieving High Efficiencies for Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells Using Silver-Free Metallization
why_relevant: Most-recent (Prog. PV 2025) survey of silver-free SHJ metallization. Cites Cao et al. 26.4% Cu-plated record and SunDrive 26.41%. Achieves 23.6% with Cu/AgCu screen print at 70% Ag savings. Key novelty-QC reference for silver-free SHJ landscape.

title: Trends in Photovoltaic Applications 2024
why_relevant: Authoritative IEA-PVPS annual trends: 1.6 TW cumulative deployed by early 2024, manufacturing capacity in China, market context for cost-driven Cu-plating adoption. Pairs with Fraunhofer ISE PV Report and ITRPV roadmap.

title: NREL Best Research-Cell Efficiency Chart
why_relevant: Canonical certified-efficiency reference (one-page chart, official NREL artifact). Authoritative citation for SHJ/HJT-IBC record efficiencies (LONGi 27.09% HBC, 27.6% HJT-IBC). Required for any plan claiming relative efficiency targets.

title: Transition from Silver- to Copper-Based Screen Printed SHJ Solar Cells
why_relevant: Latest (2025, Sol. Energy Mater. Sol. Cells) Fraunhofer ISE result: 35 µm fingers with Ag-coated-Cu, 0.1% Δη vs Ag baseline. Cites ITRPV 14 (2024). Anchors the 'screen-print Cu (not plating)' alternative — shows the *non-plating* path the hypothesis competes against.

title: IEC 61215: What It Is and Isn't (Presentation)
why_relevant: Authoritative NREL summary of the IEC 61215 qualification sequence including the 1000 h damp-heat 85C/85% RH protocol — central to validating the Ni/Cu Δη ≤ 0.5%abs threshold.

title: Reliability Testing Beyond Qualification as a Key Component in Photovoltaic's Progress Toward Grid Parity
why_relevant: Describes accelerated test sequencing beyond IEC 61215 baseline (extended damp heat, thermal cycling) — relevant to deciding whether 1000h is enough for plated Cu.

title: Photovoltaic Module Qualification Plus Testing
why_relevant: NREL Qualification Plus protocol — the de facto extension of IEC 61215 used by Fraunhofer/NREL/TÜV; defines damp heat acceptance criteria and instrumented checkpoints.

title: Non-Contact Printed Aluminum Metallization of Si Photovoltaic Devices: Preprint
why_relevant: Demonstrates TLM contact-resistivity measurement workflow on c-Si cells (10-line TLM patterns, 700–4800 µm spacing) — direct template for Ni/Cu TLM ρc < 2 mΩ·cm² acceptance.

title: Accelerated Service Life Testing of Photovoltaic Modules (Fraunhofer ISE)
why_relevant: Fraunhofer ISE methodology deck on PV module accelerated service life testing including damp heat protocol details — directly applicable to instrumenting the 1000 h chamber run.

title: Peel Testing of Ribbons on Solar Cells at Different Angles: Consistent Comparison by Using Adhesive Fracture Energies
why_relevant: Methodology for peel-test of metallization on Si cells (DIN EN 50461, IEC 61730-2 — 1 N/mm @ 90°, 50 mm/min) — the standard the Ni/Cu adhesion ≥ 1 N/mm threshold maps to.

title: Barrier Properties of Electroplating Nickel Layer for Copper Metallization
why_relevant: Open-access methodology paper on electroplating Ni as Cu diffusion barrier — composition, current density, thickness determination, and barrier efficacy testing protocol for the Ni layer of the stack.

title: Metallization and Interconnection for High-Efficiency Bifacial Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells and Modules
why_relevant: CSEM/Meyer Burger methodology review of SHJ-grade metallization (Cu plating + low-T Ag paste alternatives) — covers contact-resistivity targets and damp-heat reliability for SHJ specifically.

title: SunDrive Copper Metallisation Demonstration Project (Final ARENA Report)
why_relevant: SunDrive ARENA-funded report on commercial-scale Cu plating for SHJ — process flow, plated-stack architecture, throughput, cost (≤10% of Ag baseline), and reliability data; the closest publicly disclosed industrial protocol.

title: Getting Ahead of the Curve: Assessment of New Photovoltaic Module Reliability Risks Associated With Projected Technological Changes
why_relevant: NREL 2024 report on emerging-tech PV reliability risks including SHJ Cu metallization — identifies Cu-specific failure modes (oxidation, finger corrosion) and recommended damp-heat extension beyond 1000 h.

title: Copper(II) Sulfate Pentahydrate SDS (Fisher Scientific AC197730010, CAS 7758-99-8)
why_relevant: SDS for the primary Cu electrolyte salt (CuSO4·5H2O) — required materials-table entry for the acid Cu plating bath.

title: Sulfuric Acid ACS Reagent SDS (Fisher Scientific AC424525000, CAS 7664-93-9)
why_relevant: SDS for the H2SO4 acid component of the acid Cu plating electrolyte — bath pH ≈ 1, ~150–250 g/L H2SO4.

title: Nickel(II) Sulfamate Hydrate Product Page (Fisher Scientific AA8902018, Thermo Scientific, CAS 13770-89-3)
why_relevant: Catalog page for the primary Ni electrolyte salt — Ni(SO3NH2)2·xH2O, the industry-standard chemistry for plated Ni barrier seed.

title: Atotech Cupracid Ultra A Technical Data Sheet (Acid Cu Electroplating)
why_relevant: Atotech's commercial acid-Cu plating chemistry TDS — composition, additives, operating conditions; representative of the industrial bath used by SunDrive/CSEM-style Cu metallization lines.

title: Atotech Solar Industry — Plated Ni/Cu Cell Metallization (Product Page)
why_relevant: Atotech's PV-grade plated metallization product family overview — confirms Ni/Cu/Ag stack architecture and commercial availability of qualified chemistries.

title: DuPont Solamet PV21A Photovoltaic Metallization Paste Tech Sheet
why_relevant: Technical datasheet for a Solamet front-side Ag paste — control reagent for the Ag screen-print baseline arm of the experiment.

title: DuPont Solamet PV56S Photovoltaic Metallization Paste Data Sheet
why_relevant: DuPont Solamet PV56S datasheet (low-T paste suitable for SHJ-style architectures) — control reagent for the Ag screen-print arm.

title: Current and Future Metallization Challenges and Solutions for Crystalline Cell Manufacturing (Photovoltaics International 67)
why_relevant: Industry review covering Ag-paste pricing, plated Ni/Cu economics, and the suppliers (Heraeus, DuPont, Schlenk, Atotech, MacDermid) — key context for the cost-target sub-bullet of the hypothesis.

title: Notion Systems Solar Inkjet Equipment Overview
why_relevant: Notion Systems is one of two industrial inkjet vendors (with SUSS PiXDRO) for the plating-resist patterning step — defines the inkjet plating-mask supplier landscape.

title: Stable Copper Plated Metallization on SHJ Solar Cells & Investigation of Selective Al/AlOx Laser Patterning
why_relevant: Direct hypothesis-aligned: Fraunhofer ISE EUPVSEC 2021 conference paper showing damp-heat stability of selectively plated Cu on SHJ (NOBLE process) + Al/AlOx laser patterning. Key reliability data point for the Δη ≤ 0.5%abs threshold.

title: Metallization and Interconnection for High-Efficiency Bifacial Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells and Modules
why_relevant: CSEM/EPFL review of SHJ metallization options (Ag screen-print vs Cu plate vs SmartWire). Provides the canonical comparison baseline in the hypothesis. Open PV-Tech mirror of the trade-press article based on Photovoltaics International 41.

title: Review of the Potential of the Ni/Cu Plating Technique for Crystalline Silicon Solar Cells
why_relevant: Most-cited general reference (Materials, MDPI, OA) on Ni/Cu plating for c-Si solar cells. Covers seed-layer formation, LIP fundamentals, patterning methods, adhesion, parasitic plating. Foundational background for every plating-related entity in the wiki.

title: Reliability of Light-Induced Plated Front-Side Metallization for Crystalline Silicon Solar Cells
why_relevant: Foundational Bartsch/Glatthaar PVSC 2010 paper on light-induced plating (LIP) — the originating Fraunhofer process underpinning the SHJ Ni/Cu plating literature. Required citation for any LIP method entity in the wiki.

title: Copper Outdiffusion from Copper-Plated Solar Cell Contacts during Damp Heat Exposure
why_relevant: Critical failure-mode evidence: SIMS-confirmed Cu outdiffusion through Sn/Ag capping after damp-heat. Directly tests the hypothesis's Ni-barrier integrity claim and the SIMS metric (success criterion #4). ACS Appl. Mater. Interfaces, OSTI accepted manuscript.

title: Damp Heat Induced Degradation of Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells With Cu-Plated Contacts
why_relevant: Most-direct prior result on the exact failure scenario in the hypothesis: SHJ + Cu-plated contacts under 85/85 damp heat. IEEE J. Photovoltaics, OSTI accepted manuscript. Reports specific Δη values, Voc/Jsc/FF tracking — the same readouts the plan must produce.

title: Electroless Nickel Deposition for Front Side Metallization of Silicon Solar Cells
why_relevant: OA reference (Materials/MDPI) on electroless Ni seed-layer chemistry, NiSi/Ni2Si phase formation 300-800 C, HF activation. Directly relevant to alt-deposition mode (NiP) discussed in open question #2.

title: Metallization Techniques and Interconnection Schemes for High-Efficiency Silicon Heterojunction PV
why_relevant: PV International 41 trade article reproducing CSEM/EPFL metallization comparison for SHJ. Useful schematics + cost numbers for screen-print Ag, Cu plating, SmartWire. Public mirror of the same content as the 2018 PV-Tech article — both retained for cross-reference.

title: Native Oxide Barrier Layer for Selective Electroplated Metallization of Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells
why_relevant: Solar RRL 2019 — the canonical NOBLE paper. Demonstrates 20.0% SHJ with Cu-plated front grid, ITO contact resistivity in target range. Anchors the 'sub-100 nm Ni-barrier on TCO' state-of-art and directly informs open question #1 (continuity threshold).

title: Establishing the native oxide barrier layer for selective electroplated metallization for bifacial silicon heterojunction solar cells
why_relevant: AIP Conf. Proc. (SiliconPV 2019) follow-on to NOBLE establishing the bifacial implementation. Adds process-window data (current density, plating bath chemistry) directly usable in the experimental plan.

title: Printing Technologies for Silicon Solar Cell Metallization: A Comprehensive Review
why_relevant: Comprehensive Prog. PV 2023 review of screen/stencil/inkjet/dispense metallization — defines the Ag-screen-print baseline (cost, line width, Ag mg/cell) the hypothesis must beat. OA Wiley + Fraunhofer Publica.

title: Towards a cutting-edge metallization process for silicon heterojunction solar cells with very low silver laydown
why_relevant: Most-recent Fraunhofer ISE result (Prog. PV 2024) on the screen-printed Ag baseline pushed to 1.4 mg/W on SHJ. Sets the cost target the plated Cu hypothesis must beat by 90% (success criterion #5).

title: Pushing Heterojunction Technology Further: Novel Metallization Processes and Architectures
why_relevant: OA SiliconPV 2024 conference paper surveying SHJ metallization architectures incl. Cu plating, pattern-transfer printing, copper paste. Industry-perspective complement to Hatt/Karas academic papers. Names equipment vendors and TRL levels.

title: Challenges facing copper-plated metallisation for silicon photovoltaics: Insights from integrated circuit technology development
why_relevant: Lennon UNSW review (Prog. PV 2019) on Cu-plating challenges — would be valuable for IC-derived reliability framing but Wiley paywalled and no UNSW Unsworks OA copy located within attempt budget. Coverage substituted by Lennon 2013 (already in wiki via fetch by Subagent C: see commons references) and the Karas papers.

title: Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cells With Copper-Plated Grid Electrodes: Status and Comparison With Silver Thick-Film Techniques
why_relevant: Canonical Geissbühler IEEE J. Photovolt. 2014 paper (22.4% SHJ Cu-plated) — paywalled on IEEE Xplore, EPFL Infoscience link not located. Coverage substituted by 2018 PV-Tech / PV International articles by same EPFL/CSEM group (Faes et al.) which restate the result and add later data.

title: Copper-related light-induced degradation in crystalline silicon (doctoral dissertation)
why_relevant: Aalto PhD dissertation collecting Cu-LID lifetime measurements; quantifies how interstitial Cu in Si causes minority-carrier lifetime collapse — direct evidence for the failure mode that a leaky Ni barrier on SHJ would expose.

title: Retraction: Graded bandgap perovskite solar cells (Ergen et al., Nature Materials 16, 522-525, 2017)
why_relevant: High-profile Nature Materials retraction of a 'major advance' photovoltaic claim due to non-reproducibility of cell efficiency and time-evolution of key PV parameters. Cautionary precedent for the kind of reproducibility/reliability claims this hypothesis must withstand (1000h DH, contact resistivity, no efficiency loss).
